scope.target: Add a human-facing authoring guide plus reusable skill and sub-agent assets for first-pass problem-frame drafting
scope.dependency_radius: AI collaboration guides, skill assets, and problem-frame sub-agent prompt definitions
constraints: Keep the skill focused on drafting, not compliance claims
Make the guide useful both from documents and reverse-engineering from code
Follow existing asset and wrapper structure
non_goals: No claim that the new skill is already battle-tested in downstream projects

inputs.architecture_target: Make problem-frame authoring repeatable enough that the validator can be adopted in real projects
review_findings: The pilot added validator-ready examples but still needed an authoring path
The user wants a reusable skill or sub-agent prompt that can work from requirement/spec or code
user_constraints: Prefer assets that can later be imported into a current project
Keep the work aligned with the existing workflow

steps: Add a portable problem-frame-author skill asset
Add a canonical problem-frame-sub-agent prompt asset
Add top-level wrapper skill definitions under .claude and .agents
Update repo indexes so the new assets are discoverable
validation: The guide explains how to derive a first problem frame from requirements/specs or code
The skill and sub-agent assets clearly stop before claiming spec compliance
The new assets align with the existing repository structure
deferred_items: Runtime-specific wrappers under other agent folders if future adoption requires them
Forward-testing the new skill on real downstream project documents

results.summary: Added a first-pass problem-frame authoring guide, a reusable problem-frame-author skill asset, and a canonical problem-frame-sub-agent prompt so teams can draft validator-ready inputs from requirements/specs or code.
tests_run: Structural verification by file presence and index updates
